# Chassis

> A lightweight, decorator-driven Express + TypeScript backend starter.
> Controllers are classes with `@route`-decorated methods; exported
> controllers auto-mount; responses go through `req.resHandler`; errors
> are thrown as `AppError`; config is one validated zod schema.
> Integrations (MongoDB, Auth0, Sentry) are opt-in via environment
> variables. `npm run verify` (typecheck + lint + test) is the quality
> gate.

## Conventions for code generation

- Add an endpoint: `npm run gen <Name>`, then edit the controller methods.
- Endpoints: methods on a class extending `Routable`, decorated with
  `@route('get'|'post'|..., '/path', [middlewares])`. Export the class
  from `src/controllers/index.ts` to register it.
- Responses: `req.resHandler.ok(data)`, `.created(data)`, `.noContent()`,
  `.notFound(msg)`, `.conflict(msg)`, `.validation(issues)`. Never call
  `res.status().json()`.
- Errors: `throw new AppError(ERROR_CODES.NOT_FOUND, 'message')`. No
  try/catch in controllers — Express 5 + a central handler catch throws.
- Validation: `validate({ body: zodSchema })` in the route middleware
  array. Parsed body replaces `req.body`.
- Config: only `src/config/index.ts` reads `process.env`; it's a zod
  schema and the app exits on invalid config.
- Never edit `src/core/**` for feature work.

## Docs

- [Agent guide](AGENTS.md): conventions, do/don't, definition of done
- [Getting started](docs/getting-started.md): scaffold to running API
- [Building an API](docs/guides/building-an-api.md): a full CRUD resource
- [Authentication](docs/guides/authentication.md): Auth0 and custom providers
- [Core API reference](docs/reference/core-api.md): decorators, resHandler, AppError, validate
- [Configuration](docs/reference/configuration.md): every env var
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md): request lifecycle, decorator flow
- [Modules](docs/modules.md): the opt-in integration contract
